what: .wt cache modifier on M=256 quant scale stores (from .cg). 25+ BM confirmations at -2 to -4% M=256 improvement. Previous LB noise masked the geomean improvement. Fresh baseline v211 with more competition noise data points.
why: M=256 .wt scale stores is the most reliably beneficial single change identified across 246 versions. Write-through pushes scale data to L2 bypassing L1 write-allocate, benefiting the subsequent ASM GEMM which reads from L2. BM consistently shows -2 to -4% on M=256.
override: Combined with REDUCE_BSM=32 for split-K reduce (from 16) to improve reduce kernel M-tile size, reducing reduce grid from 1x33 to 1x33 but with 32-row tiles for better L2 reuse. REDUCE_BSM=32 never tested. Aims to stack two improvements: M=256 .wt (-2%) + split-K reduce efficiency.

what: cache_modifier=None for M=64 fused path (from .cg). For 64x7168x2048: BSM=16 BSN=128 BSK=256 NW=4 NS=2. B data per block = 16KB FP4 per K-iter, 4 iters = 64KB total. Without .cg, L1 caches 16KB B tiles across iterations. v214 tested on v211 baseline (+1.1% on old base). Re-test on v219 (.wt scale stores) base. M=64 is 2nd largest contributor to geomean at 13.1us.
why: M=64 (13.1us) is the largest remaining per-shape time. cache_modifier=None might improve L1 caching of B tiles. v103 showed -0.8% BM (neutral), v193 +1.8% LB, v214 +1.1% LB. With different baseline (.wt), the interaction might differ.

what: waves_per_eu=3 for M=64 fused path (from 2). Testing intermediate occupancy hint between 2 (current) and 4 (v129 neutral). waves=3 never tested for any fused shape. For 224 blocks (0.88 waves), waves=3 may find optimal register vs occupancy balance.
why: M=64 is the largest BM time at 13.1-13.4us. waves_per_eu=0,1,2,4 tested; waves=3 never tried for M=64. On MI355X, waves_per_eu provides occupancy hints to the compiler. The optimal value between 2 and 4 may provide better register allocation.
